name: Generative Materials Design
what: A method for designing new materials using machine learning algorithms to predict their properties.

name: Diagnostic AI
what: AI systems that assist in medical diagnosis by analyzing patient data.

name: Stellar Engineering
what: Stellar engineering involves manipulating stars to alter their properties, potentially for energy production or other purposes. This speculative technology could transform our understanding of space and energy.
source: University of California, Santa Cruz · SETI Institute

name: Dyson Swarm Milestone Roadmap
what: The Dyson Swarm is a proposed system of satellites to capture solar energy in space. This roadmap outlines the milestones needed for its development and implementation.
source: Dyson Foundation · NASA Ames Research Center

name: 4D-Printing Materials
what: 4D-printing materials can change shape over time due to external stimuli like temperature or light. This technology could enable self-assembling structures and adaptive systems.
source: Massachusetts Institute of Technology · University of California, San Diego

name: Programmable Matter
what: Programmable matter consists of particles that can change their shape and properties based on external stimuli. This technology could enable new forms of adaptive materials and structures.

name: 2D Materials Beyond Graphene (Borophene, Silicene)
what: Borophene and silicene are two-dimensional materials with unique electronic properties. These materials could revolutionize electronics, sensors, and energy storage.

name: Topological Insulators
what: Topological insulators are materials that conduct electricity on their surface while being insulating in the bulk. These materials have potential applications in quantum computing and spintronics.
source: University of California, Berkeley · Harvard University

name: MAX Phase Ceramics
what: MAX phase ceramics are a class of materials with unique properties, including high thermal stability and mechanical strength. These materials have applications in aerospace and electronics.
source: Carnegie Mellon University · Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory

name: Space-Based Solar Power
what: Space-based solar power involves deploying satellites to collect sunlight and beam it back to Earth for use. This technology could provide a constant, clean energy source.

name: Nuclear Waste Transmutation
what: Transmuting nuclear waste into less hazardous isotopes through advanced reactors or accelerator-driven systems could significantly reduce the environmental impact of nuclear energy.
source: Los Alamos National Laboratory · Oak Ridge National Laboratory

name: Iron-Air Batteries
what: Iron-air batteries use iron anodes and air cathodes to store energy, offering high energy density and low cost. This technology could revolutionize the storage of renewable energy.

name: Green Ammonia
what: Green ammonia is produced using renewable energy to power the Haber-Bosch process, reducing carbon emissions in fertilizer production and offering a sustainable fuel alternative.
source: University of California, Berkeley · NREL (National Renewable Energy Laboratory)

name: Enhanced Geothermal (EGS)
what: Enhanced geothermal systems use advanced drilling techniques to access hot rock formations and generate electricity. This technology could significantly increase the global geothermal energy supply.

name: Ocean Thermal Energy Conversion (OTEC)
what: OTEC harnesses the temperature difference between deep and surface ocean waters to generate electricity. This technology offers a sustainable energy source with minimal environmental impact.
source: Massachusetts Institute of Technology · University of Hawaii

name: Longevity Medicine (Altos Labs)
what: Longevity medicine aims to extend human lifespan through advanced biotechnologies and therapies. Altos Labs is at the forefront of developing cellular reprogramming techniques.
source: Altos Labs · Salk Institute for Biological Studies

name: Precision Oncology (Liquid Biopsies)
what: Precision oncology uses liquid biopsies to detect circulating tumor cells and DNA in blood. This technology allows for personalized cancer treatments based on real-time genetic data.
source: Baylor College of Medicine · MD Anderson Cancer Center

name: Simulation Theory Implications for Technology
what: The simulation theory suggests that our reality is a computer-generated simulation. This concept has profound implications for technology, including the development of advanced AI and virtual environments.
source: University of Oxford · MIT Media Lab

name: Digital Physics
what: A theoretical framework that posits the universe can be understood as a giant quantum computer.
source: University of California, Santa Barbara · IBM Research

name: Cross-Domain Synthesis Agent
what: Identifies hidden connections between domains and generates new insights.

name: Enhanced Geothermal Systems (EGS)
what: Creates artificial geothermal reservoirs by injecting water into hot rock formations to extract heat and generate electricity.

name: Organ-on-Chip
what: Simulates complex functions of human organs on a microscale for drug testing and disease research.

name: Longevity Medicine
what: Aims to extend human lifespan by addressing the underlying biological mechanisms of aging.

name: AI in Drug Discovery
what: Revolutionizing drug discovery by accelerating the identification of potential treatments and reducing development time.

name: AI Safety
what: Ensuring the safe and ethical operation of AI systems to prevent unintended consequences.

name: MoE Architecture
what: Mixture of Experts architecture for efficient and flexible model training using multiple specialized sub-models.

name: RAG Pipelines
what: Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipelines for producing accurate and contextually rich outputs.

name: AI Agents
what: Autonomous software entities that can perform complex tasks and interact with the environment using advanced AI techniques.

name: On-Device AI
what: Deployment of machine learning models directly on end-user devices for real-time processing and decision-making.

name: Cohere
what: Advanced conversational agents using deep learning to understand human-like text.

name: Perplexity
what: Cutting-edge AI model for natural language understanding and generation.

name: DeepSeek
what: Deep learning framework for efficient training of large language models.

name: Materials Project
what: A database and computational tool for materials discovery.

name: AI Alloy Discovery
what: AI tools for discovering new alloys with desired properties.

name: Grid Optimisation AI
what: AI systems for optimizing power grid operations.

name: Battery Chemistry AI
what: AI-driven research and development in battery chemistry.

name: Regulatory AI
what: AI systems for regulatory analysis and policy red-teaming.

name: Compliance Automation
what: Automated systems for ensuring regulatory compliance.

name: Policy Generation AI
what: AI systems for generating and optimizing public policies.

name: Protein Design AI (AlphaFold2)
what: AI methods for designing proteins with specific functions.

name: Genetic Circuit Design AI
what: AI tools for designing genetic circuits in synthetic biology.

name: Gene Therapy
what: Genetic treatments to correct or replace faulty genes.

name: CAR-T Cell Engineering
what: Engineering T-cells to target cancer cells more effectively.

name: mRNA Platforms
what: Advanced mRNA technologies for various applications.

name: Perovskite Solar
what: Efficient solar cells based on perovskite materials.

name: Solid-State Batteries
what: Batteries without liquid electrolytes, offering improved safety and performance.

name: Radiation-resistant Materials
what: Materials designed to withstand radiation exposure.

name: Aerogel Insulation
what: Lightweight, high-insulating materials for various applications.
